sunrise-sunset-js
npm GitHub
 
00:00
— · · UTC
Elevation—
Azimuth—
Daylight—

Location

Timezone is resolved automatically — no network needed.

scroll
getSunTimes · getTwilight

Anatomy of a day

Every threshold the sun crosses — dawns, dusks, golden and blue hours — computed to the second with NREL's Solar Position Algorithm. Drag the dial, or tap a card to jump there.

365 × getSunTimes

A year of light

Every spoke is one day — midnight at the core, midnight at the rim. Drag around the ring and watch the seasons reshape the sky above.

One import away

±30 s accuracy NREL SPA zero dependencies TypeScript Temporal API ready
npm install sunrise-sunset-js 
import { getSunTimes, getSolarPosition } from 'sunrise-sunset-js';

// every threshold of the day, in one call
const t = getSunTimes(51.5074, -0.1278, new Date(), {
  timezoneId: 'Europe/London',
});

t.sunrise;                              // Date
t.twilight.goldenHour.evening.start;    // Date

// live solar position
getSolarPosition(51.5074, -0.1278);
// → { elevation: 61.9, azimuth: 180.3, … }
GitHub npm NREL SPA
